title: rubus hirtus var. linguifolius
intro: Rubus hirtus var. linguifolius, also known as the hairy raspberry or linguifolius raspberry, is a variety of the raspberry plant that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is native to East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rubus hirtus var. linguifolius include its potential to protect against cardiovascular disease, improve cognitive function, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
